digest.component_or_area: MCP auth framework (composite token-verifier choke point + static bearer verifier + resource-server wiring)
propositions: Add flat scalar auth config keys to McpConfig with coercers, env vars, server.json registration, and regenerated reference docs.

A CompositeTokenVerifier acts as the single audience and fail-closed choke point: it tries verifiers in order, treats None as skip and any raise as non-acceptance, and rejects any candidate token whose resource does not equal auth_resource_server_url.

The factory built from auth_strategies enforces a closed vocabulary (static|jwt|introspection|proxy|custom) and refuses to start on unknown strategy, empty composite with auth enabled, or auth-enabled HTTP with no verifier wired.

StaticBearerVerifier parses a JSON/TOML secrets file, stores only SHA-256 digests, compares in constant time, stamps returned tokens with resource=auth_resource_server_url, and rejects records with plaintext literals or missing both token_env and token_sha256.
Wire auth into build_server so FastMCP serves the RFC 9728 PRM with a single authorization_servers entry and emits 401 plus WWW-Authenticate carrying the resource_metadata URL.

A redaction helper ensures no token or secret substring is ever emitted on any auth error or log path, and session-id replay by a different client is never served in the original principal's authenticated context.
